summary: Comprehensive vertical slice landed cleanly. Build verified by orchestrator (npm run build, 10.86s). Scope exactly matches dispatch authorization — 5 files: 1 created (MaterialLabScene.js, 868 LOC) + 4 surgical edits. No materials-foundation files touched, no GameContainer.jsx edits, no package.json edits, no TS, HMR registered top + bottom. All 12 components_landed claims verified by spot-check on diffs and grep. Observation flow + workbench gate logic correct.
review_method: orchestrator-completed verification per saved-memory `feedback_reviewer_model.md` and the reviewer-stall pattern at .claude/swarm/blockers/reviewer-stall-pattern-2026-04-26.md. The native code-quality-reviewer agent stalled at tool-use 9 mid-investigation (response was just 'Good. Now let me check sceneRegistry.js:' with no further work and no receipt). Orchestrator independently performed all checks via git diff HEAD per file + targeted grep + npm run build re-verification. Findings cross-checked against the embedded standards from the dispatch prompt.

medium.summary: Materials Lab doorway prop at (60, 130) is a small dark 38×56 rectangle with only a 🧪 emoji label above. May be subtle for a 9-year-old player to discover. Worth a floating 'MATERIALS LAB' label tween or pulsing glow in a future polish pass.

ZuzuGarageScene workbench-gate uses `aq.stepIndex >= 8` (weigh_instruction). That's intentionally permissive — lets player enter the lab early. The OR with `haveAll` (all 3 inventory samples) is also correct per dispatch. Documenting the stepIndex constant inline (or pulling from quests.js step ids) would reduce magic-number coupling if quest steps are ever reordered.

low.summary: state.observations is written as `[...prev, 'load_test_completed']` defensively (line 833-837). The questSystem.js:199-202 auto-advances observe steps unconditionally without reading observations, so this write is technically redundant for current quest flow — but it's the right defensive pattern for any future quest-UI or gating that does check `observations[]`. Worth keeping.

Chart axis label includes 'max ${xMax.toFixed(2)}' for strain ceiling — useful but could read as cluttered for non-technical players. Worker called this out as intentional in receipt notes (so player sees that wood ~0.018 is much narrower than copper ~0.35). Keep as-is unless playtest feedback requests cleaner axes.

Audio uses ui_click on test start + ui_error on failure. Both keys depend on what's in audioManifest.js — if either is missing, `audioMgr?.playSfx(key)` will be a no-op (graceful). Future audio polish could add a dedicated 'snap' SFX for steel/wood failures and a 'creak' for copper.
